title: 🐛 Bug: PWA Service Worker causing infinite API caching and outdated venue lists
body:
### Description
Users are reporting that new venues added by other members do not appear on their dashboard or map even after multiple page refreshes. The service worker is cache-first for all network fetches ending in `/api/venues`, which prevents fetching updated database entries.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open WorkSphere PWA.
2. Submit a new venue from another account or database directly.
3. Reload the dashboard on the PWA.
4. Notice the new venue is missing until the service worker is manually unregistered.

### Expected Behavior
API routes under `/api/*` should use network-first strategy, falling back to cache only when offline.

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Server-Sent Events (SSE) reconnect loop spamming server on connection drop
body:
### Description
When network connectivity fluctuates or goes offline briefly, the SSE subscription client triggers an immediate reconnection loop without any backoff or delay. This results in hundreds of rapid requests per second, exhausting server connections and causing 429 rate limit responses.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the venue detail screen with real-time crowd tracking.
2. Simulate network disconnect (e.g., toggle offline mode in DevTools).
3. Check the Network tab to see a massive flood of `/api/realtime` connection requests in a split second.

### Expected Behavior
Reconnection attempts should employ exponential backoff (e.g., starting at 1s, doubling up to a maximum of 30s) to prevent spamming the Next.js server.

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Map component crashes with TypeError when route coordinates contain null values
body:
### Description
When selecting a newly proposed venue that has missing or invalid coordinates, the road router (OSRM) service returns an empty list of route geometries. This causes React Leaflet to throw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'lat')` and crashes the entire application viewport.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Search for a venue that was recently created with coordinates `[null, null]` or malformed lat/lng.
2. Click on "Directions" or "View on Map" on the booking item.
3. The page crashes with a white screen.

### Expected Behavior
The map should gracefully handle invalid/empty routing coordinates, display a friendly toast warning "Route directions unavailable for this venue," and center the map on the user's location.

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Memory leak during rapid PDF receipt generation in serverless functions
body:
### Description
When a user generates or downloads multiple PDF receipts in rapid succession from their profile dashboard, memory usage grows linearly until the Vercel Serverless Function terminates with an Out-Of-Memory (OOM) error (exit code 137). This is caused by `pdf-lib` document instances not being dereferenced or garbage-collected properly within the serverless lifecycle.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Go to the profile page with 10+ bookings.
2. Click "Download PDF" rapidly on several bookings.
3. Observe the API route `/api/bookings/receipt` latency spike followed by an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway once the serverless container crashes.

### Expected Behavior
The PDF generation function should dispose of document streams immediately and minimize memory overhead to keep execution within serverless limits (1024MB).

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Clerk webhook handler crashes when sync event lacks profile picture
body:
### Description
When a new user signs up via Clerk using an SSO provider that does not supply a profile image URL (resulting in an empty or null `image_url` field), our webhook handler tries to read `.replace()` on the URL to optimize size, causing the webhook handler to crash with 500 Internal Server Error.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a user in Clerk without an avatar (or remove the avatar).
2. Clerk fires the `user.created` webhook.
3. The Next.js webhook endpoint crashes when parsing the avatar payload.

### Expected Behavior
Fallback to a default placeholder avatar (e.g., matching the user's initials) if `image_url` is null or undefined in the Clerk payload.

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Overpass API rate limits freeze map markers generation without error display
body:
### Description
When multiple users search at the same time, the OpenStreetMap Overpass API triggers an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests response. When this happens, our client map remains empty without warning, leading users to believe there are no workspaces available in the searched area.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Trigger multiple rapid search queries to exhaust the Overpass API token bucket.
2. Notice the search results network call returns 429 status code.
3. The UI loader spinner disappears, but the map shows no markers, and no message is displayed to the user.

### Expected Behavior
A warning banner or toast should inform the user: "High traffic detected. Please wait a few seconds and try searching again."

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Prisma Connection Pool exhaustion due to long-running Groq AI Agent responses
body:
### Description
Under load, the application suffers from database connection timeouts (`PrismaClientInitializationError: Can't reach database server...`). The Context Agent holds a database connection open while waiting for the LLM response from the Groq API (which takes 2-4 seconds). During this wait, other concurrent requests are blocked from getting a connection.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Send 15 parallel chat queries to the AI agent.
2. Inspect server logs.
3. See Prisma connection pool connection errors on the 10th+ request.

### Expected Behavior
Prisma client should release the connection back to the pool or use non-blocking querying before executing long external API calls to the LLM.

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Custom PWA Install Banner button fails to display on iOS Safari
body:
### Description
On iOS Safari, our custom PWA "Install App" banner is shown, but clicking it does nothing because iOS does not support the standard `beforeinstallprompt` event. This frustrates iOS users who expect a native installation window to open.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Load WorkSphere on an iPhone using Safari.
2. See the custom install banner appear.
3. Click the "Install" button.
4. Observe that nothing happens.

### Expected Behavior
On iOS devices, the banner should detect the Safari environment and display an overlay instructing the user to click the Share button and select "Add to Home Screen."

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Venue Rating modal submit button cut off on smaller mobile viewports
body:
### Description
On mobile screens (width < 380px), the rating modal contents overflow vertically. Because the modal wrapper is fixed and lacks scroll behavior, the "Submit Review" button goes off-screen and cannot be clicked by the user.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a venue details page on a small mobile device (e.g., iPhone SE).
2. Click on the "Rate Venue" button to open the modal.
3. Note that the layout extends past the bottom boundary, making the submit button inaccessible.

### Expected Behavior
The modal should have a max-height of 90vh and `overflow-y: auto` to allow scrolling through rating criteria on small devices.

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.

title: 🐛 Bug: Groq AI response parsing crashes when JSON is wrapped in markdown code blocks
body:
### Description
Sometimes, the Groq LLM includes markdown backticks (e.g., ```json ... ```) around its structured output instead of returning raw JSON text. When this happens, our Orchestrator fails to parse it using `JSON.parse()`, crashing the current conversation thread with a parsing exception.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Send a query that triggers complex reasoning (e.g. comparing 4 cafes).
2. Groq returns the JSON payload wrapped in a markdown code fence.
3. The backend logs show `SyntaxError: Unexpected token \` in JSON at position 0` and the chat interface displays a generic system crash message.

### Expected Behavior
The parser should detect if the LLM output is wrapped in markdown code fences, strip the backticks and language identifier, and then parse the clean JSON string.

### Screenshots / Videos
*No screenshots provided.*

### Checklist
- [x] I have searched existing issues to ensure this is not a duplicate.
